experts.title: Founder & ACSD Co-Director / Senior Localization, Land Tenure and Due Diligence Specialist
experts.bio_summary: Founder and ACSD Co-Director with 32+ years supporting design, execution, and evaluation of government, private-sector, NGO, and donor-funded programs in Burkina Faso, Mali, Niger, and Chad, specializing in partnership localization, organizational capacity assessments, land tenure/land use surveys for IDP resettlement, and project/grant due diligence.

donor_experience.notes: Chief of Party, USAID/SRO LCapS Activity - national partner census of 647 households and OCAT of 219 organizations (2024-2025); evaluation of population and institutional resilience under USAID/Burkina Faso-funded ASTER Project, Mercy Corps (2023-2025); USAID IGR Project startup (2021-2022); Women's Voice and Leadership Program baseline/mapping (2019); USAID DRG Assessment (2016); USAID regional EFSP workshop (2016); Administrative and Financial Director, Africare - USAID/FFP-funded BOFSI Program, $22M, Chad (2013-2015)
Managed three UNHCR refugee camps with a $1M budget while Administrative and Financial Director, Africare, Chad (2013-2015)

education_certifications.title: M.S., Sustainable Development and the Environment
education_certifications.institution: Universite de Ouagadougou Joseph Ki-Zerbo, Burkina Faso

education_certifications.title: M.S., Accounting and Financial Sciences and Techniques
UEMOA, World Bank, and EU Procurement Systems
USAID Sub-Recipient and Sub-Grant Management and Audit
USAID Resource Management, Finance, Audit, Administration, Grants, and Procurement

experts.title: Senior Social Safeguards and Resettlement Specialist
experts.bio_summary: Senior Social Safeguards and Resettlement Specialist with 15+ years supporting USAID, MCC, World Bank, and IFC-funded infrastructure, energy, mining, and agricultural programs across Africa, specializing in resettlement action plans, livelihood restoration, and grievance redress mechanisms under IFC Performance Standard 5 and World Bank ESS5, including work in West Africa (Benin, Burkina Faso, Guinea).

education_certifications.title: B.Sc. & M.Sc., Macroeconomics and Development Planning and Management
Advanced Training in Environmental and Social Safeguards
IFC Performance Standards Compliance and Social Audit
Independent Member, International Association for Impact Assessment

needs_review: Primary sector 'Migration and displacement' (resettlement/land acquisition focus) is a judgment call vs 'Protection' - confirm best fit.

Geography limited to Benin, Burkina Faso, and Guinea per West/Central Africa+Sahel policy; Malawi (MCA Malawi II Road Upgrading Compact, MCA Malawi Power Compact) and Tanzania/Uganda (East African Crude Oil Pipeline Project) assignments excluded as outside policy scope.

No donor_experience recorded - named projects (MCA Malawi II, MCA Benin II, MCA Malawi Power Compact) are Millennium Challenge Account/MCC-funded, and Houndé Gold Operation and Rio Tinto Simandou are private-sector mining - none are in the seeded donor list. World Bank/IFC appear only as standards/frameworks (ESS5, PS5) and a training source (World Bank Institute), not as direct project funders in the achievements listed. Confirm whether MCC should be added to the donors lookup table.

experts.title: Senior MEAL, CLA and Knowledge Management Specialist
experts.bio_summary: Senior Monitoring, Evaluation, Accountability, and Learning (MEAL) specialist with 20+ years supporting USAID and other donor-funded programs in West Africa, designing MEAL and CLA systems for complex multi-country portfolios, strengthening partner capacity, and leading WASH and resilience/food-security knowledge management in Burkina Faso and Niger.

donor_experience.notes: Knowledge Management, MEAL & CLA Coordinator, USAID-funded SCC Program ($16M), Mercy Corps, Burkina Faso and Niger (2020-2024), covering 42 RISE II implementing partners
Documentation & Communication Officer, Gates Foundation-funded INSPIRE Project ($21M), IntraHealth International, multi-country (2018-2020)
EU-funded WASH MEAL baselines as part of national WASH MEAL systems strengthening for WaterAid Burkina Faso (2013-2018)
UNICEF-funded WASH MEAL baselines and data verification for WaterAid Burkina Faso (2013-2018)
Led baseline studies, needs assessments, and evaluations for GIZ-funded projects among other donors (short-term consultancies, 2003-2025)

needs_review: Primary sector 'MEAL technical integration' chosen given the role's core focus; secondary sectors ('WASH' for WaterAid programs, 'Food security' for RISE II resilience portfolio) reflect the project mix - confirm best fit.

Geography limited to Burkina Faso and Niger (named countries for SCC/RISE II and WaterAid programs) per policy; the Gates Foundation-funded INSPIRE Project's 'seven countries' are not individually named in the CV and were not recorded.
Conrad Hilton Foundation (mentioned as a funder of baseline/evaluation work) not added as a donor tag - not in TOR/RFP/proposal donor list (per project policy).

experts.title: Senior USAID Executive / Operations and Program Management Specialist
experts.bio_summary: Accomplished senior USAID executive with 20+ years of leadership in humanitarian relief, stabilization, disaster relief, and development operations across Africa, Asia, and Eastern Europe, with deep expertise in executive management, operations oversight, HR/procurement, and crisis response, including regional mission leadership across West Africa (USAID/Senegal) and a country directorship in Chad.

donor_experience.notes: Supervisory Executive Officer, USAID/Cambodia (2024-2025); Supervisory Regional EXO, USAID/Senegal/Tbilisi directing operations for 12 missions across West Africa and the Caucasus (2016-2024); Country Director, USAID-funded PDEV II Project executed by IRD, Chad ($65M, 2014-2015); Regional Representative/Senior Development Foreign Service Officer, USAID/OTI Afghanistan ($97M stabilization program, 2010-2012)

education_certifications.title: B.A., Psychology

experts.title: Senior Agricultural Engineer / Rural Infrastructure and Food Security Specialist
experts.bio_summary: Experienced agricultural engineer with 30+ years designing, executing, and evaluating agricultural and rural infrastructure (roads, wells, dams, irrigated perimeters) under Food-for-Work programming and micro-finance/livelihoods projects to strengthen social cohesion in conflict- and extractive-industry-affected areas of Chad, Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, and the Central African Republic.

donor_experience.notes: Series of USAID/FFP Food Security Programs, Africare, Chad (Ouaddai, Batha, Sila Regions) - Country Director/Technical Advisor/Project Coordinator/Agronomist/MEAL Coordinator roles (1998-2015); M&E support for three USG-funded refugee camps (Gaga, Farchana, Gore, 2005-2014)
Food-for-Work (FFW) programming sourced from USG and WFP for rural infrastructure (roads, wells, dams, irrigated perimeters) in Chad, Mali, Burkina Faso, and Niger
Agronomist/Agricultural Extension Officer/MEAL Specialist, German-funded Projet de Developpement Rural Ouaddai/Biltine - Amenagement des Ouadis, executed by GTZ, North-East Chad (1993-1994)

Co-facilitated pilot of FED (Fonds Europeen de Developpement)-funded self-managed credit fund (Caisse Al-Amane) benefiting three women's unions, Ouaddai Region, Chad, within the 1998-2015 Africare Title II program
